note: HIDDEN gaps — implied, not stated. A summarizer that faithfully lists the facts will typically NOT surface these; catching them requires reasoning (arithmetic, timeline logic, domain knowledge, cross-references). 'Caught' = the output explicitly flags the problem, not merely restates the underlying facts.

H1.desc: Launch locked for June 1 but legal review 'takes ~3 weeks' and hasn't started as of May 20 — cannot finish in time. The core contradiction nobody in the room caught.
Payments integration depends on the vendor API that 'might slip a week' — building the launch on an unready dependency.
Demo video editor booked until the 30th, but dry run is May 31 / launch June 1 — the video may not be ready in time.

H2.desc: Rep committed to send the SOC 2 report + DPA — a real deliverable buried mid-conversation, easy to lose.
Priya 'drives' the decision but the COO must actually sign — an approval dependency that isn't obvious.
Wants to be live within six weeks tied to fiscal year end — a hard, budget-linked deadline.

H3.desc: Cancelling the old CRM 'saves 2000/mo' but the replacement costs 2400/mo — it's a net INCREASE of ~400/mo, framed as a saving.
Board deck, investor update, AND conference flight all fall on the same Friday — can't do all three; travel conflicts with producing the deck.
Decided last week to pause hiring, now reopening the eng role — reverses a very recent decision.
AWS bill up 40%, 'someone should look into it' — nobody assigned.

H4.desc: Mandate is 300k. Proposed cuts sum to 100k+80k+60k = 240k (and they keep the 40k event) — 60k short of target.
Sales travel is exempt, so the 80k travel saving is overstated (only non-sales counts) — the real shortfall is even larger than 60k.
Contractor cuts may break the two projects that depend on them — raised but unresolved, and it's a real delivery risk.

H5.desc: Ibuprofen (an NSAID) ordered for a patient just started on warfarin — a well-known dangerous bleeding interaction. The highest-stakes hidden gap.
Potassium 5.9 is high (hyperkalemia); 'will address' but no concrete plan or owner.
Diaz's discharge depends on the morning chest x-ray but 'someone needs to make sure it gets ordered' — ownerless and blocking.
Diaz's discharge also depends on home oxygen setup that RT 'was supposed to' arrange — unconfirmed dependency that could delay discharge.
